root_motivation: Heavy build sessions die via SIGKILL (cgroup teardown) before the SessionEnd hook fires, leaving ctrace NDJSON logs permanently un-summarized. The gap is measurable (18 missing summaries as of 2026-05-28), grows silently, and corrupts the session-postmortem join that depends on a complete summary record. A reusable single-pass Rust engine with a backfill subcommand closes the gap idempotently and replaces the fragile 6-pass shell renderer.
user_persona: Joe Yen on his Arch Linux laptop; runs ctrace sessions daily, relies on session summaries for self-review and session-postmortem pipeline. Runs `scribe backfill` periodically or from cron to keep the summary record complete.

acceptance_criteria.description: scribe render <log.ndjson> writes <log>.summary.md containing the Log: line, the duration/event/PID/write count line, and all five section headers (Top binaries, Writes outside expected scope, Deletions, Outbound connect, and Flagged when any flagged write exists).
For a fixture log with no flagged writes, the Flagged section is omitted. For a fixture log with a /home/jsy/.ssh/ write, the Flagged section is present and lists it.
scribe render on a 124k-event fixture completes in <= 2 seconds and makes a single file pass (verified structurally — no second open of the same file path).
A truncated final line (valid prefix, no closing brace) does not fail the render; the summary is produced and reports '1 malformed line skipped'.
scribe backfill <dir> renders exactly the *.ndjson files missing a *.summary.md and leaves existing summaries untouched; prints 'rendered N, skipped M' count.
scribe backfill <dir> run twice in a row renders 0 on the second run (idempotent).
scribe backfill <dir> --dry-run writes no files and exits 0, listing the would-render set.
scribe backfill --force <dir> re-renders a log whose summary is older than the ndjson (mtime comparison).
--help documents render and backfill with their flags; exits 0.
Rendering a non-existent log exits non-zero with a usage error; an empty dir backfills 0 and exits 0.

scope: Single-pass NDJSON streaming renderer for ctrace session logs
scribe render subcommand: one log to one summary.md
scribe backfill subcommand: idempotent bulk render of a directory
Output parity with summarize-ctrace-session.sh (same sections, same in_scope/flag_paths regexes)
non_goals: No daemon/watch mode; purely batch processing
No schema evolution of ctrace NDJSON (parses the current format only)
Does not replace or modify the existing SessionEnd hook or shell script
No web UI, TUI, or interactive mode
No network egress of any kind
Does not manage or rotate ctrace log files

additional.no_async_runtime: sync I/O only; tokio/async-std are banned dependencies

five_whys_trace.q: Why do we need a Rust binary instead of fixing the shell script?
five_whys_trace.a: The shell script makes 6 full passes (1 awk + 5 jq) over each file. For 18+ files or a 300-file rollup, that becomes jq storms and ARG_MAX hits. The real problem is the SessionEnd hook never fires on SIGKILL'd sessions, so no backfill mechanism exists at all — we need a new tool.

five_whys_trace.q: Why do sessions get SIGKILL'd instead of SIGTERM'd gracefully?
five_whys_trace.a: Heavy build/kernel sessions run as headless services under claude-build.service's cgroup. When the /build tick ends, the cgroup is torn down and all children receive SIGKILL — no chance to run the SessionEnd hook. Memory note: self_build_detached_cgroup_teardown.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is the gap in summaries a real problem rather than a cosmetic one?
five_whys_trace.a: The session-postmortem pipeline (visions/continuity.md) joins ctrace summaries with four other substrates. Missing summaries corrupt the join and hide what happened in the most resource-intensive sessions — exactly the ones most worth reviewing.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What exactly does 'output parity' mean — pixel-perfect or functionally equivalent?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Functionally equivalent: same sections present/absent under the same conditions, same in_scope and flag_paths regex sets. Whitespace/formatting details are not mandated.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the render subcommand also support --out - (stdout)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes, per PRD: --out PATH flag, and --out - for stdout.
